description: A main-process IPC handler that performs file, shell, or network operations on renderer-supplied data without validation, enabling path traversal or injection.
body: Failure mode: an ipcMain handler takes a file path or command argument from the renderer and acts on it directly, so a malicious or compromised renderer can read/write arbitrary paths or inject arguments via the privileged main process. Detection signals: a handler concatenates renderer input into a path or command; no type/shape/path validation; tests can escape the intended directory. Do not trust IPC input. Safer fallback: validate payload types and shapes, constrain file paths to an allow-listed directory, verify the sender where relevant, and reject anything unexpected before performing the privileged operation.
